title: LCP Ruby Permission Definition
description: Schema for permission metadata that defines role-based access control. Each permission file maps to one model and defines what each role can do: CRUD operations, field visibility, data scopes, and record-level rules. Defined in config/lcp_ruby/permissions/.

model.description: The model this permission applies to. Must match a model name from config/lcp_ruby/models/.

default_role.description: Role assigned to users who don't match any other role. Falls back to denying all access if omitted.

roles.description: Role definitions. Keys are role names, values define what the role can do.

field_overrides.description: Per-field access overrides that apply across roles. Useful for restricting sensitive fields (e.g., salary) to specific roles without duplicating the field list in every role.

record_rules.description: Record-level rules that deny CRUD operations based on field conditions. Evaluated per-record at runtime (e.g., deny editing archived records).

crud_action.description: A CRUD operation. 'restore' and 'permanently_destroy' are only relevant for soft-deleted models.

role_config.description: Access configuration for a single role.

crud.description: List of CRUD operations this role can perform.

field_access.description: Which fields this role can read and write.

readable.description: Fields this role can see. 'all' grants access to every field.

writable.description: Fields this role can modify. 'all' grants write access to every field. Empty array means read-only.

field_list.description: 'all' for unrestricted access, or an explicit array of field names.

actions_access.description: Custom action permissions. 'all' grants access to every custom action. Object form allows explicit allowed/denied lists.

allowed.description: 'all' or array of action names this role can execute.

denied.description: Action names explicitly denied for this role (takes precedence over allowed).

scope_config.description: Data scope limiting which records this role can see. 'all' means no restriction. Object form defines the scoping strategy.

type.description: Scoping strategy. 'field_match' compares a record field to a value. 'association' filters by user's associated records. 'where' applies raw conditions. 'custom' delegates to host app.

field.description: For field_match/association: the column on the model to filter by.

value.description: For field_match: the value to match against. Use 'current_user_id' for the logged-in user's ID.

user_field.description: For association: the field on the user record to read the matching value from.

method.description: For association: method to call on the user to get associated values (e.g., user.department_ids).

conditions.description: For where: raw conditions hash passed to ActiveRecord where().

presenter_access.description: Which presenters this role can access for the model. 'all' grants access to every presenter. Array form limits to specific presenter names.

field_override.description: Per-field access override applied across all roles.

readable_by.description: Only these roles can read this field. Other roles see it as hidden.

writable_by.description: Only these roles can modify this field. Other roles see it as read-only.

masked_for.description: These roles see a masked value (e.g., '****') instead of the real value.

record_rule.description: A record-level access rule that denies specific CRUD operations when a field condition is met. Automatically hides edit/destroy buttons on matching records.

name.description: Rule identifier for debugging and error messages.

rule_condition.description: Field condition evaluated per-record. Supports all 12 condition operators.

field.description: Field name on the record to evaluate.

value.description: Value to compare against. Type depends on the operator (string, number, array for 'in'/'not_in', omitted for 'present'/'blank').

rule_effect.description: What happens when the rule condition matches.

deny_crud.description: CRUD operations denied on matching records.

except_roles.description: Roles exempt from this rule (e.g., admin can still edit archived records).
